texto: You cannot let a fear of failure, or a fear of comparison, or a fear of judgement stop you from doing what's going to make you great. You cannot succeed without this risk of failure, you cannot have a voice without the risk of criticism and you cannot love without the risk of loss. You must go out and take these risk. … Do what’s uncomfortable, and scary, and hard, but pays off in the long run. Be willing to fail. Let yourself fail. Fail in the way and the place where you would want to fail. Fail, pick yourself up and fail again. Because without this struggle, what is your success anyway?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Charlie Day que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “You cannot let a fear of failure, or a fear of comparison, or a fear of judgement stop you from d…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, greatness, happiness, com foco em inspiracao e luto.

texto: Thinking of Plan B muddies up your chances of succeeding at Plan A.
explicacao: Citação de Charlie Day sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Thinking of Plan B muddies up your chances of succeeding at Plan A.” articula reflexao, thinking, plan em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como plan e succeeding.
